relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Deadness in misalignment is the starting condition.
The age and its air-ruler shaped the old walk.
Flesh-pattern desire once governed everyone.
But the Divine is rich in mercy and great in love.
Even dead in trespasses, we are made alive together with Messiah by grace.
We are raised and seated together in Messiah.
Coming ages display the surpassing riches of grace-kindness.
Grace saves/heals through trust as Divine gift.
Boasting is excluded.
We are Divine workmanship, created for prepared good works.
The nations remember former flesh-bound exclusion language.
They were once alienated from Israel’s citizenship and promise-covenants.
In Messiah, the far ones are brought near.
Messiah himself is peace, tearing down the dividing wall.
The two are created in him as one new human.
Both are reconciled in one body through the cross.
Peace is announced to far and near.
Both have access in one Spirit to the Father.
Strangers become fellow citizens and household members.
The household is built on apostles and prophets, Messiah the cornerstone.
The whole building grows into holy temple.
Together they become a Spirit-dwelling for the Divine.

contemplative_note: Ephesians 2 is the one-new-humanity chapter: death becomes life, distance becomes nearness, wall becomes peace, strangers become household, and the body becomes Divine dwelling.
